authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Mill Creek Reach A (Lassen NP boundary to canyon-mouth gauging station)
reaches.toDesc: U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Mill Creek Canyon

reaches.name: Mill Creek Reach B (canyon-mouth gauging station to the mouth)
reaches.fromDesc: U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Mill Creek Canyon
reaches.toDesc: Mouth (confluence with the Sacramento River, near Los Molinos)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(64) — Mill Creek (Tehama Co.)
sources.quotedText: (64) Mill Creek (Tehama Co.). (A) From the Lassen National Park boundary downstream to the U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Mill Creek Canyon. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) From U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Mill Creek Canyon downstream to the mouth of Mill Creek. Jun. 16 through Sep. 30. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — footnote definition of hatchery trout/steelhead (**)
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

sources.title: Mill Creek Instream Flow Study (Tehama County) — California Department of Fish and Wildlife
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Open the last Saturday in April through November 15, from the Lassen National Park boundary downstream to the gauging station at the mouth of Mill Creek Canyon.
regulations.verbatimText: From the Lassen National Park boundary downstream to the U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at the mouth of Mill Creek Canyon. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, from the Lassen NP boundary downstream to the canyon-mouth gauging station.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, from the Lassen NP boundary downstream to the canyon-mouth gauging station.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

regulations.humanSummary: Open June 16 through September 30, from the canyon-mouth gauging station downstream to the mouth of Mill Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: From U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Mill Creek Canyon downstream to the mouth of Mill Creek. Jun. 16 through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, from the canyon-mouth gauging station downstream to the mouth.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
